title: Electromagnetic-connection gravity — single lattice dielectric K(x)

D-EM1_eikonal_viability._desc: single scalar: factor-1 redshift AND factor-2 bend? (exact, algebraic)

D-EM2_single_field_deflection.note: one dielectric field reproduces factor-2 (≈4); the rest leg on the same field is factor-1 (≈2); ratio≈2 cancels lattice error.
D-EM2_single_field_deflection._desc: single dielectric K(x) on lattice: K_bend≈4 (no 2nd field eqn); rest leg≈2

D-EM3_radiation_as_source.note: equal energy ⇒ equal gravity under EM-connection (ratio→1); rest-leg coupling sources nothing from massless field energy (fraction→0). This is the physical fork.
D-EM3_radiation_as_source._desc: standing (E,B) energy shell deflects like equal-energy rest mass (the fork)

D-EM-D1_flat_regression.note: dielectric stepper ≡ two free Weyl walks at zero field (= F62 D1).
D-EM-D1_flat_regression._desc: flat dielectric stepper ≡ two free Weyl walks bit-for-bit (= F62 D1)

D-EM-D2a_dielectric_freefall.note: EP carried by rest leg √A; dielectric B=1/A is the locked partner.
D-EM-D2a_dielectric_freefall._desc: equivalence principle on a dielectric Rindler frame (= F62 D2a)

D-EM-D2b_dynamical_redshift.note: deep dielectric clock runs slow at rate √A=e^{-u}; canonical exp form.
D-EM-D2b_dynamical_redshift._desc: dielectric clock rate ∝ √A=(1−u), measured from zitterbewegung (= F62 D2b)

D-EM-D2c_dielectric_deflection.note: one dielectric field (B=1/A) gives Einstein factor-2 K_eik≈4; F62 needs a separately-sourced second leg B=1−2Φ/c².
D-EM-D2c_dielectric_deflection._desc: fast packet past a dielectric mass: K_bend≈4 from ONE field (cf F62 two-leg)

D-EM-D3a_backreaction_norm.note: self-sourced dielectric metric evolves; matter norm conserved.
D-EM-D3a_backreaction_norm._desc: self-gravitating dielectric packet: norm conserved as its own K(x) evolves

D-EM4_redshift_bend_consistency.note: ONE self-sourced dielectric K: factor-1 redshift (deep clock slow, matches √A=(1−u) to <4%) AND factor-2 bend (index slope exactly 2× rest-leg) — the dynamical close of the D-EM1 loop, the F64 counterpart of F62-D3a self-redshift.
D-EM4_redshift_bend_consistency._desc: ONE self-sourced K: factor-1 redshift AND factor-2 bend together (closes D-EM1)

D-EM5_derive_dielectric.derived: epsilon = mu = K  (=> A=1/K, B=K, AB=1)

fdtd_reflection.note: matched ≈ grid floor (reflectionless proper rotation); mismatched ≈ Fresnel (scalar contamination).

D-EM5_derive_dielectric.note: ε=μ=K is DERIVED (proper rotation ⇒ impedance match ⇒ ε=μ; index ⇒ =K; factor-1 redshift ⇒ conformal factor ⇒ AB=1). The EM sector (conformally invariant) carries BOTH metric legs as one scalar; only the clock/redshift selects the conformal representative. Impedance-matched placement is reflectionless on the lattice; the non-dielectric placements (refractive-only, clock-only) reflect — that reflected wave IS the F26 scalar contamination.
D-EM5_derive_dielectric._desc: DERIVE ε=μ=K (⇒A=1/K,B=K) from proper (E,B) rotation: impedance+conformal+redshift

D-EM6_light_bends_light.note: A propagating Maxwell pulse deflects toward a lens made of pure (E,B) field energy — light bends light, dynamically. Factor-2 ratio = 2 (Einstein, not Newton). The lens has zero rest mass, so the rest-leg route (F50/F52) sources nothing from it (0): the decisive fork, now on a real wave rather than an eikonal probe.
D-EM6_light_bends_light._desc: dynamical Maxwell pulse deflected by a pure-EM-energy lens (light bends light)

D-EM7_absolute_deflection_3d.note: absolute Einstein factor-2 (K_bend→4) on a genuine 3-D ray through the true-1/r dielectric; finite-field approach from above. Removes the 2-D logarithmic-Poisson caveat (Finding 8) of D-EM6.
D-EM7_absolute_deflection_3d._desc: absolute K_bend→4 on a genuine 3-D ray (true 1/r; removes the 2-D log caveat)

D-EM8_dynamical_field.note: Φ is a dynamical field: static limit = the Poisson well that sources K; disturbances propagate at finite c_g (causal gravity, vs the instantaneous Poisson of F52/F62); free field conserves energy (a kinetic term ⇒ Lagrangian element + dielectric GWs).
D-EM8_dynamical_field._desc: Φ as a dynamical field: static=Poisson well, finite c_g (causal), energy conserved

D-EM9_strong_field_ppn.verdict: F64's redshift-fixed K=(1−u)^-2 matches GR at 1st order (γ=1, light bending 4GM/bc²) but has β=1/2 ⇒ Mercury 50.1″/cy (16.7% excess) — EXCLUDED by MESSENGER. The impedance-matched exponential K=e^{2u} (agreeing with the linear fix at O(u)) restores β=1 ⇒ GR-identical. The 2nd-PPN order selects the nonlinear completion D-EM5's linear derivation left open.
D-EM9_strong_field_ppn.note: The classic tests: light bending depends only on γ (=1 for the dielectric ⇒ passes); perihelion depends on β too (=1/2 ⇒ fails unless K is completed to e^{2u}). A concrete falsifiable prediction and a correction to the model's nonlinear sector.
D-EM9_strong_field_ppn._desc: PPN vs Schwarzschild: γ=1 (light OK) but β=½ for (1−u)⁻²; e^{2u} restores GR

D-EM10_pin_G_to_cell_scale.note: 4πG is NOT free: the 4π is the exact lattice Green's function (F58), and G is the Sakharov-induced value pinned by the cell scale a=√(2πη g_*)d^{1/4}ℓ_P=3.81 ℓ_P (η=1/12 F61, g_*=16) with 1/G∝√d (F59). The dielectric inherits the induced coupling the rest-leg route (F52) left posited. Residual freedom: the gauge sector's contribution to g_* (flagged in F61).
D-EM10_pin_G_to_cell_scale._desc: 4πG not free: 4π lattice-exact, G induced (a=3.81 ℓ_P, 1/G∝√d) per F58/F59/F61

D-EM11_coevolving_self_redshift.note: fully co-evolving packets (spreading through the real gradient) — no fixed-A workaround; deep clock slower, Hilbert-measured. Residual ~10% is the finite packet sampling the well curvature (the precise coefficient is D-EM4's localized clock). F64 counterpart of F62-D3a self-redshift.
D-EM11_coevolving_self_redshift._desc: fully co-evolving self-redshift (Hilbert clock, no fixed-radius workaround)
